verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored exclusively to the cytoplasmic/inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. It has no transmembrane domain and no extracellular exposure. All published localization data place LYN at the intracellular face of the PM (and perinuclear/Golgi region), with additional pools in the nucleus and endomembranes. No isoform or post-translational mechanism delivers LYN to the outer leaflet. It is not the target of any surface-directed antibody, ADC, or CAR-T program. Small-molecule kinase inhibitors access it intracellularly. The protein body is entirely intracellular.
